fields.Name: Novi AMS
fields.Description: Novi AMS connector — syncs members, events, ecommerce, custom fields, webhooks, blogs, site content, NPS surveys, and catalog items via the Novi REST API (HTTP Basic with raw API key, per-tenant base URL https://www.<assoc>.org/api/).

Configuration.AuthNotes: The Novi API key is used as-is in the Authorization header: "Authorization: Basic <apiKey>". The raw API key is the credential value — this is NOT standard HTTP Basic Auth (base64 of user:password). The API key is generated in the Novi admin UI (Settings > API Keys) and can be scoped to specific member groups and fields. HTTPS is required; HTTP is refused.

TenantBaseURLContract.description: Novi AMS has no shared API host. Every tenant runs on their own website domain. The connector reads TenantBaseURL from per-connection configuration (CompanyIntegration.Configuration.TenantBaseURL) at runtime. All API calls go to {TenantBaseURL}/{resource-path}. Example: https://www.myassociation.org/api/members

PaginationDefaults.notes: Offset-based pagination across all list endpoints. Query params: pageSize (number of records), offset (0-indexed start position). Not all lookup/reference endpoints paginate — smaller reference sets (MemberTypes, EventCategories, CommitteeRoles, CreditTypes, BlogCategories, GroupCategories) may return complete lists without pagination.

universalPK.notes: UniqueID (GUID/string) is the universal primary key across all Novi AMS resources. InternalIdentifier (integer) is a secondary internal ID present on some resources. Runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier will validate per-IO.

IncrementalSyncCapability.notes: No universal incremental watermark field. Each resource uses its own date filter parameter: Members use lastModifiedDate; Committees use lastUpdatedDateFrom/lastUpdatedDateTo; Activities use dateFrom/dateTo; Membership Activities use dateFrom/dateTo; Articles use publishSinceDate; Attendees use registrationDate or lastModifiedDate. IncrementalWatermarkField is set per Integration Object accordingly.

Configuration.WebhooksNotes: Webhook subscription management is available via CRUD on /api/webhooks (GET list, GET detail, POST create, PUT update, DELETE). Webhook delivery (outbound HTTP POST from Novi to a registered endpoint) is an outbound delivery mechanism and is out of scope for this connector. Webhook event types observed: customer.created, customer.updated. Full event type catalog is not documented in the public collection.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNotes: No bulk/batch endpoints are documented in the Postman collection. All operations are single-record. POST create and PUT update operate on individual records.
Configuration.APIVersioningNotes: No API versioning scheme is documented in the Postman collection or help articles. The base URL path does not contain a version segment (e.g. /v1/). The API is versioned implicitly by the tenant Novi site version.
Configuration.TokenRefreshStrategyNotes: API key authentication has no token expiry or refresh cycle. API keys are long-lived admin-generated credentials managed in the Novi admin UI.

ErrorResponseShape.notes: Not explicitly documented in the Postman collection. Based on collection descriptions, errors are returned as HTTP 4XX status codes. The exact error response body shape is not specified in the public documentation. The connector should handle HTTP status-code-based error detection.

PUTSemantics.description: All PUT requests via the Novi API are full updates. The connector must GET the current state of a record, apply modifications, and PUT the entire object back. Sending a partial object will null out unspecified fields. Exception: Custom Activity Type PUT preserves unspecified fields (non-standard behavior documented in the collection).
PUTSemantics.exception: Custom Activity Type PUT (/api/custom-activity-types/:id) does NOT follow full-object-replacement — it preserves fields not included in the request body.

Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPatternNotes: Novi AMS does not expose customer-defined custom objects at the API level. Custom field definitions are accessible via /api/custom-fields (system-level) and /api/customer-custom-fields (per-member values). There is no customer-creatable object type analogous to HubSpot custom objects.
Configuration.CustomFieldMarkerPatternNotes: Custom field definitions are at /api/custom-fields (GET list). Per-member custom field values are at /api/customer-custom-fields (GET list, GET detail, PUT). Custom fields are a separate resource family, not embedded markers on member records.

WriteCapability.notes: Write capability varies by resource. Members: POST create, PUT update (full-object replacement), no hard-delete (soft-delete via PUT Active=false). Events/Tickets/Registrations/Orders/NPS Surveys: POST create, PUT update. Activities: POST create, PUT update, DELETE. Webhooks: full CRUD including DELETE. Articles/Static Pages: full CRUD including DELETE. Committee Members: POST create, PUT update, DELETE. Resource-level capability flags are set per Integration Object.

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNotes: No ETag, If-Match, If-Unmodified-Since, or version-number fields are documented in the Postman collection or help articles. Conflict resolution must use snapshot-3-way only.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNotes: Mixed delete semantics by resource type. Members cannot be hard-deleted — the documented pattern is to set Active=false via PUT (soft delete / archive). Activities, Committee Members, Webhooks, Articles, and Static Pages support HTTP DELETE (hard delete). Member records with Active=false remain in the API response with Active=false flag. Connector must check Active status for member-type objects.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionReasoning: Read-only (Pull) by default per policy. Write-path (bidirectional) requires sandbox validation. Full-object-replacement PUT semantics make accidental data loss a risk — a connector must GET-then-PUT correctly or unspecified fields are nulled out. This must be validated against a sandbox before enabling bidirectional.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionReasoning: No ETag or version field — MostRecent is not reliably safe. DestWins is the conservative default when bidirectional is eventually enabled. Full-object-replacement PUT semantics require extra care; DestWins avoids overwriting destination changes with stale source data.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.name: SSO / OAuth2 / JWT login (member auth, not data)
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Novi SSO (JWT and OAuth2/OpenID Connect) is a member authentication flow, not a data sync surface. The SSO endpoints issue tokens for end-user portal login. They do not expose syncable data records. Covered by help.noviams.com SSO articles (sources 5–8 in SOURCES.json) which are explicitly marked INFORMATIONAL.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.name: QuickBooks-direct integration route
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Novi AMS has a native QuickBooks integration that syncs financial data (orders carry QBSalesReceiptID/QBInvoiceID fields; members carry quickbooksID). This integration route runs directly between Novi and QuickBooks, not via the REST API connector. Exposing QuickBooks data through the Novi connector would duplicate a native integration path and create data ownership ambiguity.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.name: Zapier route
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Novi AMS offers a Zapier integration for workflow automation. Zapier is a separate integration pathway with its own trigger/action model. It is not a REST API surface for data sync and is out of scope per task specification.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.name: API-feed / webhook DELIVERY (near-real-time signal, not a sync stream)
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: The Novi API Feed (help.noviams.com/en/articles/2331476) is an outbound affiliate member-sync delivery mechanism — Novi pushes member data to affiliate orgs. Webhook delivery (the outbound HTTP POST from Novi to a registered endpoint) is similarly an outbound signal. Neither is a queryable REST resource. Webhook MANAGEMENT (CRUD on /api/webhooks) IS in scope as a syncable object family.

Configuration.RateLimitModelNotes: Rate limits are per API key: 20 calls/second (burst), 600 calls/minute (sustained), 100,000 calls/day. BatchMaxRequestCount=10/BatchRequestWaitTime=1s is set conservatively to honor the 600/min sustained limit (600/60=10/s). The 20/s is a burst ceiling; connectors should target the sustained rate to avoid daily limit exhaustion on large syncs.
